The data presented in the table offers a comprehensive overview of the various factors influencing the study outcomes across
different sites. Each factor is meticulously analyzed to understand its statistical significance, as indicated by the associated p-
values. This analysis is crucial for determining which variables exhibit a reliable impact on the observed phenomena. For
instance, the column labeled "HR" (Hazard Ratio) provides insight into the direction and magnitude of the effect of each factor
on the primary outcome, while the "95% CI" (95% Confidence Interval) offers a range within which the true effect size is likely
to fall, thereby reflecting the precision of the estimates. The varied p-values highlight the differing levels of statistical
significance across factors, allowing for a nuanced interpretation of the results. Understanding these metrics is essential for
drawing valid conclusions about the relationships between the identified factors and the outcomes at each study site. This
analysis not only underscores the importance of statistical rigor in research but also aids in guiding future studies and potential
interventions based on the observed trends within the data.
